sections.narration: minerals are basically not adding much here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. it brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. contains useful polyphenols. adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. very simple ingredient identity. a clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

displayItems.title: polyphenol reputation is a real plus
displayItems.explanation: It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile.

displayItems.title: contains useful polyphenols
displayItems.explanation: Adds extra value beyond the core fat chemistry alone.

displayItems.title: very simple ingredient identity
displayItems.explanation: A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

sections.subtitleText: the upsides first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. it brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. contains useful polyphenols. adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. very simple ingredient identity. a clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: extremely energy dense. still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast. quality varies a lot. source and authenticity matter in practice. easy to overpour without noticing. a healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.

closing.summary: Extra Virgin Olive Oil has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
